name: ValidateMultiTransactionLimits
businessLogic: Check if limit evaluation is enabled via configuration flag; if disabled, return immediately.
Determine the feature type from slotsDTO (MultiCreditTransaction, MultiDebitTransaction, or FundTransfer); skip if not a supported multi-leg transaction type.

Read the appropriate multi-leg DTO from slots based on feature type: MultiCreditTransactionDTO (MULTI_CREDIT_DETAILS), MultiDebitTransactionDTO (MULTI_DEBIT_DETAILS), or TransferDTO (TRANSFER_DETAILS); return if DTO is missing or has no tranLines.
Read the list of TransferLegContexts from slots (TRANSFER_LEG_CONTEXTS); skip if absent or empty.
For each TxnLineDTO leg in tranLines: determine operation type (DEPOSIT for CREDIT indicator, WITHDRAWAL for DEBIT indicator); skip legs with null or unrecognised creditDebitIndicator.
For each leg, find the matching TransferLegContext by account number and creditDebitIndicator; skip if no matching context or no ReferenceContractDTO is found.
Retrieve the applicable operational limits from the leg's ReferenceContractDTO: depositOpLimits for DEPOSIT, withdrawalOpLimits for WITHDRAWAL; skip leg if no limits configured.
Partition operational limit names into AMOUNT-suffixed and COUNT-suffixed lists; warn on unrecognised suffixes.
Build limit codes by combining purposeId, operationType, and limit field name (uppercased) for both AMOUNT and COUNT categories.

Build per-leg txn event ObjectNodes for amount and/or count evaluations, populating txnDate, txnAmount, currencyCode, txnCount, serviceCode, customerId, accountId, paymentChannel, financialFlag, and a random txnId.
Collect all LegEvaluation objects (one per valid leg) into a list and pass them to LimitEvaluationServiceUtil.evaluateAllLegs() for atomic evaluation within a single transactional boundary.

If any limit external reference IDs are returned, store them in slots under LIMIT_EXTERNAL_REF_IDS and store the transaction value date under LIMIT_REVERSAL_TXN_DATE for potential downstream reversal.
